I've uploaded a code repository in the directory /testbed. Consider the following PR description:

<pr_description>
Auto sorting items not inside Unsorted zone causes camera/view to stay centered on Unsorted Zone
### Describe the bug

When trying to sort items, I had the items to be sorted on the wrong tile that is not within the Unsorted Zone. I pressed (O) to sort all items, and the view became stuck within the center where the Unsorted Zone was when I started the action. I can still move, but the view does not follow.
One way I found to “fix” this bug in-game is by hauling items into the appropriate tile within the defined Unsorted Zone and then pressing (O) again to sort the items. This fixes the view and properly sorts the items.
After further testing, I found that this also happens when sorting items that are inside the Unsorted Zone but do not have any defined zones that fit them. For example, if there is only a Spare Parts zone but no Books or Default zone, it will sort planks correctly. However, if you try to sort a book without a specific Books or Default zone, the issue occurs even if the item is inside the Unsorted Zone.

### Attach save file

[Waverly-trimmed.tar.gz](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/26713075/Waverly-trimmed.tar.gz)

### Steps to reproduce

The save file attached has already been prepared, so just follow the steps.

1. After loading into the save, press O to sort the items below the character.
2. The view should be centered onto the Unsorted Zone, but nothing happens. The character will still be on the tile and able to move, but the view will not center on the character.

"Fixing" it in-game:

1. Haul the items one step north, where the Unsorted Zone is.
2. Press O to sort the items.
3. The items should be sorted inside the refugee center within the prepared Default Zone and the camera will return to centering on the player again, even when moving.


### Expected behavior

Nothing should happen when trying to sort items when the items are inside the wrong tiles while having already set up the proper zones. There should only be an error popup or something similar.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### Versions and configuration

- OS: Android
    - OS Version: Manufacturer: ITEL; Model: itel A663LC; Release: 13; Incremental: GL-250516V1115; 
- Game Version: cdda-experimental-2026-04-13-1304 a518665-dirty [64-bit]
- Graphics Version: Tiles
- Game Language: System language []
- Mods loaded: [
    Dark Days Ahead [dda]
]

### Additional context

_No response_

   168	namespace
   169	{
   170	faction_id _fac_id( Character &you )
   171	{
   172	    faction const *fac = you.get_faction();
   173	    return fac == nullptr ? faction_id() : fac->id;
   174	}
   175	} // namespace
   176	
   177	static bool is_valid_study_book( const item &it, const Character &you,
   178	                                 const std::set<skill_id> *skill_prefs )
   179	{
   180	    if( it.has_var( "activity_var" ) && it.get_var( "activity_var" ) != you.name ) {
   181	        return false;
   182	    }
   183	    if( you.check_read_condition( it ) != read_condition_result::SUCCESS ) {
   184	        return false;
   185	    }
   186	    // if zone has skill preferences, filter by them
   187	    if( skill_prefs && it.is_book() && it.type->book ) {
   188	        const skill_id &book_skill = it.type->book->skill;
   189	        if( book_skill == skill_id::NULL_ID() ) {
   190	            return false;
   191	        }
   192	        if( skill_prefs->count( book_skill ) == 0 ) {
   193	            return false;
   194	        }
   195	    }
   196	    return true;
   197	}
   198	
   199	static item_location find_study_book( const tripoint_abs_ms &zone_pos, Character &you )
   200	{
   201	    map &here = get_map();
   202	    zone_manager &mgr = zone_manager::get_manager();
   203	    const zone_data *zone = mgr.get_zone_at( zone_pos, zone_type_STUDY_ZONE, _fac_id( you ) );
   204	    const std::set<skill_id> *skill_prefs = nullptr;
   205	    if( zone && zone->has_options() ) {
   206	        const study_zone_options *options = dynamic_cast<const study_zone_options *>
   207	                                            ( &zone->get_options() );
   208	        if( options ) {
   209	            skill_prefs = options->get_skill_preferences( you.name );
   210	        }
   211	    }
   212	    const tripoint_bub_ms zone_loc = here.get_bub( zone_pos );
   213	
   214	    // items on the ground
   215	    for( item &it : here.i_at( zone_loc ) ) {
   216	        if( is_valid_study_book( it, you, skill_prefs ) ) {
   217	            return item_location( map_cursor( zone_loc ), &it );
   218	        }
   219	
   220	        // books in containers
   221	        if( it.has_pocket_type( pocket_type::CONTAINER ) ) {
   222	            for( item *contained : it.all_items_container_top() ) {
   223	                if( is_valid_study_book( *contained, you, skill_prefs ) ) {
   224	                    item_location container_loc( map_cursor( zone_loc ), &it );
   225	                    return item_location( container_loc, contained );
   226	                }
   227	            }
   228	        }
   229	    }
   230	    return item_location();
   231	}
   232	
   233	/** Activity-associated item */
   234	struct act_item {
   235	    /// inventory item
   236	    item_location loc;
   237	    /// How many items need to be processed
   238	    int count;
   239	    /// Amount of moves that processing will consume
   240	    int consumed_moves;
   241	
   242	    act_item( const item_location &loc, int count, int consumed_moves )
   243	        : loc( loc ),
   244	          count( count ),
   245	          consumed_moves( consumed_moves ) {}
   246	};

   251	{

   253	        npc *guy = dynamic_cast<npc *>( &you );
   254	        if( guy ) {
   255	            guy->revert_after_activity();
   256	        }
   257	    }

   772	namespace zone_sorting
   773	{
   774	
   775	// Cache routes computed by route_length() for reuse by route_to_destination().
   776	// Avoids recomputing the same A* when the sorter probes route distance and
   777	// then immediately routes to the same destination.
   778	namespace
   779	{
   780	struct zone_route_cache {
   781	    static constexpr size_t max_entries = 200;
   782	
   783	    tripoint_bub_ms start;
   784	    object_type grab_type = object_type::NONE;
   785	    tripoint_rel_ms grab_point;
   786	    int arm_str = 0;
   787	    units::mass veh_mass = 0_gram;
   788	    // destination -> route. Empty route means unreachable.
   789	    std::unordered_map<tripoint_bub_ms, std::vector<tripoint_bub_ms>> entries;
   790	    // FIFO eviction order -- front is oldest.
   791	    std::deque<tripoint_bub_ms> insertion_order;
   792	    bool initialized = false;
   793	
   794	    void ensure_valid( const Character &who ) {
   795	        object_type gt = object_type::NONE;
   796	        tripoint_rel_ms gp;
   797	        int cur_arm_str = 0;
   798	        units::mass cur_veh_mass = 0_gram;
   799	        if( who.is_avatar() ) {
   800	            gt = who.as_avatar()->get_grab_type();
   801	            gp = who.as_avatar()->grab_point;
   802	            if( gt == object_type::VEHICLE ) {
   803	                map &here = get_map();
   804	                const tripoint_bub_ms veh_pos = who.pos_bub() + gp;
   805	                const optional_vpart_position ovp = here.veh_at( veh_pos );
   806	                if( ovp && ovp->vehicle().get_points().size() == 1 ) {
   807	                    cur_arm_str = who.get_arm_str();
   808	                    cur_veh_mass = ovp->vehicle().total_mass( here );
   809	                }
   810	            }
   811	        }
   812	        if( !initialized || start != who.pos_bub() || grab_type != gt ||
   813	            grab_point != gp || arm_str != cur_arm_str || veh_mass != cur_veh_mass ) {
   814	            start = who.pos_bub();
   815	            grab_type = gt;
   816	            grab_point = gp;
   817	            arm_str = cur_arm_str;
   818	            veh_mass = cur_veh_mass;
   819	            entries.clear();
   820	            insertion_order.clear();
   821	            initialized = true;
   822	        }
   823	    }
   824	
   825	    const std::vector<tripoint_bub_ms> *find( const tripoint_bub_ms &dest ) const {
   826	        auto it = entries.find( dest );
   827	        if( it != entries.end() ) {
   828	            return &it->second;
   829	        }
   830	        return nullptr;
   831	    }
   832	
   833	    void store( const tripoint_bub_ms &dest, std::vector<tripoint_bub_ms> route ) {
   834	        auto it = entries.find( dest );
   835	        if( it != entries.end() ) {
   836	            it->second = std::move( route );
   837	            return;
   838	        }
   839	        while( entries.size() >= max_entries && !insertion_order.empty() ) {
   840	            entries.erase( insertion_order.front() );
   841	            insertion_order.pop_front();
   842	        }
   843	        entries.emplace( dest, std::move( route ) );
   844	        insertion_order.push_back( dest );
   845	    }
   846	};
   847	
   848	zone_route_cache g_route_cache;
   849	} // namespace
   850	
   851	bool route_to_destination( Character &you, player_activity &act,
   852	                           const tripoint_bub_ms &dest, zone_activity_stage &stage )
   853	{
   854	    map &here = get_map();
   855	    std::vector<tripoint_bub_ms> route;
   856	
   857	    // Check if route_length() already computed a path for this destination.
   858	    g_route_cache.ensure_valid( you );
   859	    bool used_grab_routing = false;
   860	    bool from_cache = false;
   861	    if( const std::vector<tripoint_bub_ms> *cached = g_route_cache.find( dest ) ) {
   862	        if( !cached->empty() ) {
   863	            route = *cached;
   864	            from_cache = true;
   865	            used_grab_routing = ( you.is_avatar() &&
   866	                                  you.as_avatar()->get_grab_type() == object_type::VEHICLE );
   867	        }
   868	    }
   869	
   870	    if( route.empty() && !from_cache ) {
   871	        // Use grab-aware A* when dragging a single-tile vehicle - searches over
   872	        // (position, grab_direction) state so both player and cart can move.
   873	        if( has_grabbed_single_tile_vehicle( you, here ) ) {
   874	            // Single-tile vehicle: use grab-aware A*. If no path found,
   875	            // treat as unreachable (don't fall back to player-only routing
   876	            // which would cause cart collisions).
   877	            used_grab_routing = true;
   878	            route = route_with_grab( here, you, pathfinding_target::adjacent( dest ) );
   879	        } else {
   880	            route = here.route( you, pathfinding_target::adjacent( dest ) );
   881	        }
   882	    }
   883	
   884	    add_msg_debug( debugmode::DF_ACTIVITY,
   885	                   "route_to_dest: dest=(%d,%d) %s route_len=%zu %s",
   886	                   dest.x(), dest.y(),
   887	                   from_cache ? "cached" :
   888	                   ( used_grab_routing ? "grab_astar" : "normal_astar" ),
   889	                   route.size(),
   890	                   route.empty() ? "FAILED" : "OK" );
   891	
   892	    if( route.empty() ) {
   893	        add_msg( m_info, _( "%s can't reach the source tile.  Try to sort out loot without a cart." ),
   894	                 you.disp_name() );
   895	        return false;
   896	    }
   897	    // set the destination and restart activity after player arrives there
   898	    // note: we don't need to check for safe mode,
   899	    // the activity will be restarted only if player arrives on destination tile
   900	    stage = DO;
   901	    you.set_destination( route, act );
   902	    you.activity.set_to_null();
   903	    return true;
   904	}
   905	
   906	bool sort_skip_item( Character &you, const item *it,
   907	                     const std::vector<item_location> &other_activity_items,
   908	                     bool ignore_favorite, const tripoint_abs_ms &src,
   909	                     bool *spillable_skipped )
   910	{
   911	    const zone_manager &mgr = zone_manager::get_manager();
   912	
   913	    // skip unpickable liquid
   914	    if( !it->made_of_from_type( phase_id::SOLID ) ) {
   915	        return true;
   916	    }
   917	
   918	    // skip nonempty spillable containers -- picking them up triggers the
   919	    // interactive liquid dialog, which is inappropriate during automated
   920	    // sorting.  Normal pickup also refuses these (pickup.cpp, ACT_INSERT_ITEM).
   921	    if( it->is_bucket_nonempty() ) {
   922	        if( spillable_skipped ) {
   923	            *spillable_skipped = true;
   924	        }
   925	        return true;
   926	    }
   927	
   928	    // don't steal disassembly in progress
   929	    if( it->has_var( "activity_var" ) ) {
   930	        return true;
   931	    }
   932	
   933	    // don't steal items from other activities, e.g. crafts in progress
   934	    if( std::find_if( other_activity_items.begin(), other_activity_items.end(),
   935	    [&it]( const item_location & activity_it ) {
   936	    return activity_it.get_item() == it;
   937	    } ) != other_activity_items.end() ) {
   938	        return true;
   939	    }
   940	
   941	    // skip items not owned by you
   942	    if( !it->is_owned_by( you, true ) ) {
   943	        return true;
   944	    }
   945	
   946	    if( it->is_favorite && ignore_favorite ) {
   947	        return true;
   948	    }
   949	
   950	    const faction_id fac_id = _fac_id( you );
   951	    const zone_type_id zt_id = mgr.get_near_zone_type_for_item( *it, you.pos_abs(),
   952	                               MAX_VIEW_DISTANCE, fac_id );
   953	    // Skip items already at their destination regardless of whether the zone
   954	    // is bound to terrain or vehicle cargo. Delivery tries cargo first, so
   955	    // items often land in vehicle storage even at terrain-bound zones (e.g.,
   956	    // a fridge appliance on a LOOT_FOOD tile). Binding-agnostic check
   957	    // prevents infinite re-sort loops in that situation.
   958	    if( zt_id != zone_type_LOOT_CUSTOM && zt_id != zone_type_LOOT_ITEM_GROUP &&
   959	        mgr.has( zt_id, src, fac_id ) ) {
   960	        return true;
   961	    }
   962	    if( ( zt_id == zone_type_LOOT_CUSTOM || zt_id == zone_type_LOOT_ITEM_GROUP ) &&
   963	        mgr.custom_loot_has( src, it, zt_id, fac_id ) ) {
   964	        return true;
   965	    }
   966	
   967	    return false;
   968	}
   969	
   970	unload_sort_options set_unload_options( Character &you, const tripoint_abs_ms &src,
   971	                                        bool use_zone_type )
   972	{
   973	    const zone_manager &mgr = zone_manager::get_manager();
   974	    unload_sort_options zone_sort_options;
   975	
   976	    std::vector<zone_data const *> const zones = mgr.get_zones_at( src, zone_type_UNLOAD_ALL,
   977	            _fac_id( you ) );
   978	
   979	    // set rules from all zones in stack
   980	    for( zone_data const *zone : zones ) {
   981	        if( !zone->get_enabled() ) {
   982	            continue;
   983	        };
   984	        unload_options const &options = dynamic_cast<const unload_options &>( zone->get_options() );
   985	        zone_sort_options.unload_molle |= options.unload_molle();
   986	        zone_sort_options.unload_mods |= options.unload_mods();
   987	        zone_sort_options.unload_always |= options.unload_always();
   988	
   989	        zone_sort_options.unload_sparse_only |= options.unload_sparse_only();
   990	        if( options.unload_sparse_only() &&
   991	            options.unload_sparse_threshold() > zone_sort_options.unload_sparse_threshold ) {
   992	            zone_sort_options.unload_sparse_threshold = options.unload_sparse_threshold();
   993	        }
   994	
   995	        if( use_zone_type ) {
   996	            zone_sort_options.ignore_favorite |= zone->get_type() == zone_type_LOOT_IGNORE_FAVORITES;
   997	            zone_sort_options.unload_all |= zone->get_type() == zone_type_UNLOAD_ALL;
   998	            zone_sort_options.unload_corpses |= zone->get_type() == zone_type_STRIP_CORPSES;
   999	        }
  1000	    }
  1001	    return zone_sort_options;
  1002	}
  1003	
  1004	zone_items populate_items( const tripoint_bub_ms &src_bub )
  1005	{
  1006	    map &here = get_map();
  1007	    const std::optional<vpart_reference> vp = here.veh_at( src_bub ).cargo();
  1008	
  1009	    zone_items items;
  1010	    // Collect items from both vehicle cargo and ground at this tile.
  1011	    // The bool in each pair tracks whether the item is from vehicle cargo.
  1012	    if( vp ) {
  1013	        for( item &it : vp->items() ) {
  1014	            items.emplace_back( &it, true );
  1015	        }
  1016	    }
  1017	    for( item &it : here.i_at( src_bub ) ) {
  1018	        items.emplace_back( &it, false );
  1019	    }
  1020	    return items;
  1021	}
  1022	
  1023	bool ignore_contents( Character &you, const tripoint_abs_ms &src )
  1024	{
  1025	    const zone_manager &mgr = zone_manager::get_manager();
  1026	    // check ignorable zones for ignore_contents enabled
  1027	    for( const auto &zone_type : ignorable_zone_types ) {
  1028	        // add zones using mgr.get_zones_at
  1029	        for( zone_data const *zone : mgr.get_zones_at( src, zone_type, _fac_id( you ) ) ) {
  1030	            ignorable_options const &options = dynamic_cast<const ignorable_options &>( zone->get_options() );
  1031	            if( options.get_ignore_contents() ) {
  1032	                return true;
  1033	            }
  1034	        }
  1035	    }
  1036	    return false;
  1037	}
  1038	
  1039	bool ignore_zone_position( Character &you, const tripoint_abs_ms &src,
  1040	                           bool ignore_contents )
  1041	{
  1042	    const zone_manager &mgr = zone_manager::get_manager();
  1043	    map &here = get_map();
  1044	    const tripoint_bub_ms src_bub = here.get_bub( src );
  1045

  1047	           ignore_contents ||
  1048	           here.get_field( src_bub, fd_fire ) != nullptr ||
  1049	           !here.can_put_items_ter_furn( src_bub ) ||
  1050	           here.impassable_field_at( src_bub );
  1051	}
  1052	
  1053	bool has_items_to_sort( Character &you, const tripoint_abs_ms &src,
  1054	                        unload_sort_options zone_unload_options,
  1055	                        const std::vector<item_location> &other_activity_items,
  1056	                        const zone_items &items, bool *pickup_failure,
  1057	                        bool *spillable_skipped )
  1058	{
  1059	    const zone_manager &mgr = zone_manager::get_manager();
  1060	    const faction_id fac_id = _fac_id( you );
  1061	    const tripoint_abs_ms &abspos = you.pos_abs();
  1062	
  1063	    *pickup_failure = false;
  1064	
  1065	    // Any UNSORTED zone at src (terrain or vehicle) makes all items at that
  1066	    // tile eligible for sorting. The terrain/vehicle distinction only matters
  1067	    // at the destination (where items get placed), not at the source.
  1068	    const bool src_has_unsorted = mgr.has( zone_type_LOOT_UNSORTED, src, fac_id );
  1069	    const bool src_has_vehicle_unsorted = mgr.has_vehicle( zone_type_LOOT_UNSORTED, src, fac_id );
  1070	
  1071	    // When grabbed cart sits on the source tile, items stay in cart cargo
  1072	    // (virtual pickup) so the player carry capacity check doesn't apply.
  1073	    bool virtual_pickup_available = false;
  1074	    if( you.is_avatar() && you.as_avatar()->get_grab_type() == object_type::VEHICLE ) {
  1075	        const tripoint_bub_ms cart_pos = you.pos_bub() + you.as_avatar()->grab_point;
  1076	        if( get_map().get_abs( cart_pos ) == src ) {
  1077	            virtual_pickup_available = get_map().veh_at( cart_pos ).cargo().has_value();
  1078	        }
  1079	    }
  1080	
  1081	    // When the grabbed cart is at a terrain-only unsorted zone (no vehicle
  1082	    // zone), it's being used for transport - don't re-sort its cargo.
  1083	    // If there IS a vehicle zone on the cart, the user explicitly wants
  1084	    // the cart's cargo sorted.
  1085	    const bool skip_cart_cargo = virtual_pickup_available && !src_has_vehicle_unsorted;
  1086	
  1087	    for( std::pair<item *, bool> it_pair : items ) {
  1088	        if( !src_has_unsorted ) {
  1089	            continue;
  1090	        }
  1091	        if( it_pair.second && skip_cart_cargo ) {
  1092	            continue;
  1093	        }
  1094	
  1095	        item *it = it_pair.first;
  1096	        const zone_type_id dest_zone_type_id = mgr.get_near_zone_type_for_item( *it, abspos,
  1097	                                               MAX_VIEW_DISTANCE, fac_id );
  1098	
  1099	        if( dest_zone_type_id == zone_type_id::NULL_ID() ) {
  1100	            continue;

 13438	void zone_sort_activity_actor::update_other_activity_items()
 13439	{
 13440	    other_activity_items.clear();
 13441	
 13442	    //store other activity items so they aren't sorted later
 13443	    for( const npc &guy : g->all_npcs() ) {
 13444	        if( !guy.activity.targets.empty() ) {
 13445	            for( const item_location &target : guy.activity.targets ) {
 13446	                other_activity_items.push_back( target );
 13447	            }
 13448	        }
 13449	    }
 13450	    for( const item_location &target : get_player_character().activity.targets ) {
 13451	        other_activity_items.push_back( target );
 13452	    }
 13453	}
 13454	
 13455	void zone_sort_activity_actor::do_turn( player_activity &act, Character &you )
 13456	{
 13457	    update_other_activity_items();
 13458	    // Save viewport state before base call. For NPCs, revert_after_activity()
 13459	    // replaces the activity and destroys this actor before returning.
 13460	    const bool had_viewport = viewport_was_active;
 13461	    const int saved_zoom = viewport_saved_zoom;
 13462	    zone_activity_actor::do_turn( act, you );
 13463	
 13464	    // True completion: activity nulled AND no auto-move destination pending.
 13465	    // route_to_destination sets destination before nulling; true end does not.
 13466	    // Use saved locals since this may be destroyed.
 13467	    if( act.is_null() && !you.has_destination() && had_viewport ) {
 13468	        if( !test_mode ) {
 13469	            g->set_zoom( saved_zoom );
 13470	            g->mark_main_ui_adaptor_resize();
 13471	        }
 13472	        if( you.is_avatar() ) {
 13473	            you.as_avatar()->zone_sort_viewport = {};
 13474	        }
 13475	    }
 13476	}
 13477	
 13478	void zone_sort_activity_actor::canceled( player_activity &, Character &you )
 13479	{
 13480	    restore_viewport( you );
 13481	}
 13482	
 13483	void zone_sort_activity_actor::restore_viewport( Character &you )
 13484	{
 13485	    if( !viewport_was_active || !you.is_avatar() ) {
 13486	        return;
 13487	    }
 13488	    if( !test_mode ) {
 13489	        g->set_zoom( viewport_saved_zoom );
 13490	        g->mark_main_ui_adaptor_resize();
 13491	    }
 13492	    you.as_avatar()->zone_sort_viewport = {};
 13493	    viewport_was_active = false;
 13494	}
 13495	
 13496	void zone_sort_activity_actor::stage_init( player_activity &, Character &you )
 13497	{
 13498	    zone_manager &mgr = zone_manager::get_manager();
 13499	    mgr.cache_avatar_location();
 13500	    coord_set.clear();
 13501	    unreachable_sources.clear();
 13502	    const faction_id fac = you.get_faction_id();
 13503	    const bool skip_personal = !you.is_avatar() && mgr.has_personal_zones();
 13504	    for( const tripoint_abs_ms &p :
 13505	         mgr.get_near( zone_type_LOOT_UNSORTED, you.pos_abs(), MAX_VIEW_DISTANCE, nullptr, fac ) ) {
 13506	        if( skip_personal && !mgr.has_nonpersonal( zone_type_LOOT_UNSORTED, p, fac ) ) {
 13507	            continue;
 13508	        }
 13509	        coord_set.insert( p );
 13510	    }
 13511	
 13512	    if( you.is_avatar() && !coord_set.empty() ) {
 13513	        viewport_was_active = true;
 13514	        viewport_saved_zoom = g->get_zoom();
 13515	
 13516	        zone_sorting::viewport_bbox bbox = zone_sorting::calc_zone_bbox( coord_set );
 13517	        int target = zone_sorting::calc_target_zoom(
 13518	                         bbox.width(), bbox.height(),
 13519	                         TERRAIN_WINDOW_WIDTH, TERRAIN_WINDOW_HEIGHT,
 13520	                         viewport_saved_zoom, viewport_saved_zoom );
 13521	
 13522	        avatar::zone_sort_viewport_t &vp = you.as_avatar()->zone_sort_viewport;
 13523	        vp.active = true;
 13524	        vp.center = bbox.centroid;
 13525	        vp.target_zoom = target;
 13526	        vp.bbox_min = bbox.min_corner;
 13527	        vp.bbox_max = bbox.max_corner;
 13528	        if( !test_mode ) {
 13529	            g->set_zoom( target );
 13530	            g->mark_main_ui_adaptor_resize();
 13531	        }
 13532	    }
 13533	
 13534	    stage = THINK;
 13535	}
 13536	
 13537	bool zone_sort_activity_actor::stage_think( player_activity &act, Character &you )
 13538	{
 13539	    const map &here = get_map();
 13540	    zone_manager &mgr = zone_manager::get_manager();
 13541	    mgr.cache_avatar_location();
 13542	
 13543	    num_processed = 0;
 13544	    virtual_pickup_active = false;
 13545	    picked_up_this_pass = false;
 13546	    // Clear stale state from previous source. Fresh destinations are computed
 13547	    // in stage_do when items are picked up from the new source.
 13548	    dropoff_coords.clear();
 13549	    picked_up_stuff.clear();
 13550	    drag_worst_tile.reset();
 13551	
 13552	    // Clear unreachable_sources when position or grab state changed -
 13553	    // both affect which tiles are reachable. force_clear_unreachable handles
 13554	    // fresh construction and deserialization (default values could match state).
 13555	    const object_type cur_grab_type = you.is_avatar() ?
 13556	                                      you.as_avatar()->get_grab_type() : object_type::NONE;
 13557	    const tripoint_rel_ms cur_grab_point = you.is_avatar() ?
 13558	                                           you.as_avatar()->grab_point : tripoint_rel_ms::zero;
 13559	    if( force_clear_unreachable ||
 13560	        you.pos_abs() != last_think_position ||
 13561	        cur_grab_type != last_think_grab_type ||
 13562	        cur_grab_point != last_think_grab_point ) {
 13563	        unreachable_sources.clear();
 13564	        force_clear_unreachable = false;
 13565	    }
 13566	    last_think_position = you.pos_abs();
 13567	    last_think_grab_type = cur_grab_type;
 13568	    last_think_grab_point = cur_grab_point;
 13569	
 13570	    // Sort sources by A* route distance. Pre-sort by Chebyshev (lower bound),
 13571	    // compute A* lazily, stop when chebyshev > best_route + 1.
 13572	    // Tiles past the cutoff are appended in Chebyshev order as fallback.
 13573	    std::vector<std::pair<int, tripoint_abs_ms>> candidates;
 13574	    candidates.reserve( coord_set.size() );
 13575	    for( const tripoint_abs_ms &p : coord_set ) {
 13576	        if( unreachable_sources.count( p ) ) {
 13577	            continue;
 13578	        }
 13579	        candidates.emplace_back( square_dist( you.pos_abs(), p ), p );
 13580	    }
 13581	    std::sort( candidates.begin(), candidates.end(),
 13582	    []( const std::pair<int, tripoint_abs_ms> &a, const std::pair<int, tripoint_abs_ms> &b ) {
 13583	        return a.first < b.first;
 13584	    } );
 13585	
 13586	    std::vector<std::pair<int, tripoint_abs_ms>> computed;
 13587	    computed.reserve( candidates.size() );
 13588	    std::vector<tripoint_abs_ms> oob_tiles; // out-of-bounds, handled by iteration loop
 13589	    int best_route = INT_MAX;
 13590	    size_t cutoff = candidates.size();
 13591	
 13592	    for( size_t i = 0; i < candidates.size(); i++ ) {
 13593	        const int cheb = candidates[i].first;
 13594	        if( best_route != INT_MAX && cheb > best_route + 1 ) {
 13595	            cutoff = i;
 13596	            break;
 13597	        }
 13598	        const tripoint_bub_ms p_bub = here.get_bub( candidates[i].second );
 13599	        if( !here.inbounds( p_bub ) ) {
 13600	            // Skip OOB tiles here - route_with_grab can't clip them.

 13600	            // Skip OOB tiles here - route_with_grab can't clip them.
 13601	            // The iteration loop's !inbounds() handler will route closer.
 13602	            oob_tiles.emplace_back( candidates[i].second );
 13603	            continue;
 13604	        }
 13605	        if( cheb <= 1 ) {
 13606	            // Already adjacent - no pathfinding needed
 13607	            computed.emplace_back( 0, candidates[i].second );
 13608	            best_route = 0;
 13609	        } else {
 13610	            const int dist = zone_sorting::route_length( you, p_bub );
 13611	            // Budget: each route probe costs 1 move to prevent unbounded
 13612	            // A* work in a single frame. Charged before the INT_MAX check
 13613	            // because unreachable probes explore the full search space.
 13614	            you.mod_moves( -1 );
 13615	            if( you.get_moves() <= 0 ) {
 13616	                if( dist != INT_MAX ) {
 13617	                    computed.emplace_back( dist, candidates[i].second );
 13618	                } else {
 13619	                    unreachable_sources.emplace( candidates[i].second );
 13620	                }
 13621	                cutoff = i + 1;
 13622	                break;
 13623	            }
 13624	            if( dist == INT_MAX ) {
 13625	                unreachable_sources.emplace( candidates[i].second );
 13626	                continue;
 13627	            }
 13628	            computed.emplace_back( dist, candidates[i].second );
 13629	            best_route = std::min( best_route, dist );
 13630	        }
 13631	    }
 13632	
 13633	    // Sort the computed portion by actual route distance
 13634	    std::sort( computed.begin(), computed.end(),
 13635	    []( const std::pair<int, tripoint_abs_ms> &a, const std::pair<int, tripoint_abs_ms> &b ) {
 13636	        return a.first < b.first;
 13637	    } );
 13638	
 13639	    // Build final list: route-sorted, then remaining in Chebyshev order, then OOB tiles
 13640	    std::vector<tripoint_abs_ms> src_sorted;
 13641	    src_sorted.reserve( candidates.size() );
 13642	    for( const auto &entry : computed ) {
 13643	        src_sorted.emplace_back( entry.second );
 13644	    }
 13645	    for( size_t i = cutoff; i < candidates.size(); i++ ) {
 13646	        if( !unreachable_sources.count( candidates[i].second ) ) {
 13647	            src_sorted.emplace_back( candidates[i].second );
 13648	        }
 13649	    }
 13650	    for( const tripoint_abs_ms &p : oob_tiles ) {
 13651	        src_sorted.emplace_back( p );
 13652	    }
 13653	
 13654	    // iterate over zone positions and look for items to move
 13655	    for( const tripoint_abs_ms &src : src_sorted ) {
 13656	        placement = src;
 13657	
 13658	        const tripoint_bub_ms src_bub = here.get_bub( src );
 13659	        if( !here.inbounds( src_bub ) ) {
 13660	            if( zone_sorting::sorter_out_of_bounds( you, zone_sort_activity_actor() ) ) {
 13661	                return false;
 13662	            }
 13663	            if( !zone_sorting::route_to_destination( you, act, src_bub, stage ) ) {
 13664	                unreachable_sources.emplace( src );
 13665	                continue;
 13666	            }
 13667	            return false;
 13668	        }
 13669	
 13670	        bool ignore_contents = zone_sorting::ignore_contents( you, src );
 13671	
 13672	        if( zone_sorting::ignore_zone_position( you, src, ignore_contents ) ) {
 13673	            coord_set.erase( src );
 13674	            continue;
 13675	        }
 13676	
 13677	        zone_sorting::unload_sort_options zone_unload_options = zone_sorting::set_unload_options( you, src,
 13678	                true );
 13679	
 13680	        const zone_sorting::zone_items items = zone_sorting::populate_items( src_bub );
 13681	
 13682	        // check if there is valid destination for any item of the tile
 13683	        bool pickup_failure;
 13684	        bool spillable_skipped = false;
 13685	        bool has_items_to_work_on = zone_sorting::has_items_to_sort( you, src, zone_unload_options,
 13686	                                    other_activity_items, items, &pickup_failure, &spillable_skipped );
 13687	
 13688	        if( pickup_failure && !pickup_failure_reported ) {
 13689	            pickup_failure_reported = true;
 13690	            add_msg_if_player_sees( you,
 13691	                                    _( "At least one item to be sorted is too large/heavy for %s to sort.  "
 13692	                                       "Emptying the inventory and freeing up the hands will allow for more efficient sorting." ),
 13693	                                    you.disp_name() );
 13694	        }
 13695	
 13696	        if( spillable_skipped && !spillable_skip_reported ) {
 13697	            spillable_skip_reported = true;
 13698	            you.add_msg_if_player( m_info,
 13699	                                   _( "Some open containers were not sorted to avoid spilling." ) );
 13700	        }
 13701	
 13702	        if( !has_items_to_work_on ) {
 13703	            coord_set.erase( src );
 13704	            continue;
 13705	        }
 13706	
 13707	        bool is_adjacent_or_closer = square_dist( you.pos_bub(), src_bub ) <= 1;
 13708	        if( !is_adjacent_or_closer ) {
 13709	            add_msg_debug( debugmode::DF_ACTIVITY,
 13710	                           "zone_sort THINK: routing to source (%d,%d,%d) from (%d,%d)",
 13711	                           src.x(), src.y(), src.z(),
 13712	                           you.pos_bub().x(), you.pos_bub().y() );
 13713	            // Route to source. Can't skip: post-cutoff fallback tiles weren't pre-evaluated by route_length.
 13714	            if( !zone_sorting::route_to_destination( you, act, src_bub, stage ) ) {
 13715	                add_msg_debug( debugmode::DF_ACTIVITY,
 13716	                               "zone_sort THINK: route to source FAILED, trying next" );
 13717	                continue;
 13718	            }
 13719	            return false;
 13720	        }
 13721	        add_msg_debug( debugmode::DF_ACTIVITY,
 13722	                       "zone_sort THINK: adjacent to source (%d,%d,%d), entering DO",
 13723	                       src.x(), src.y(), src.z() );
 13724	        stage = DO;
 13725	        break;
 13726	    }
 13727	    if( stage != DO && !coord_set.empty() ) {
 13728	        // Remaining entries are filtered by unreachable_sources, have
 13729	        // full destinations, or were past the route probe cutoff.
 13730	        // Clear so the activity ends cleanly instead of re-scanning.
 13731	        coord_set.clear();
 13732	    }
 13733	    return true;
 13734	}

 13736	void zone_sort_activity_actor::return_items_to_source( Character &you,
 13737	        const tripoint_bub_ms &src_bub )
 13738	{
 13739	    // Purge stale item_locations (can expire if items merged during multi-turn pickup)
 13740	    auto stale = std::remove_if( picked_up_stuff.begin(), picked_up_stuff.end(),
 13741	    []( const item_location & loc ) {
 13742	        return !loc.get_item();
 13743	    } );
 13744	    if( stale != picked_up_stuff.end() ) {
 13745	        add_msg_debug( debugmode::DF_ACTIVITY,
 13746	                       "zone_sort return_items: purged %zu stale item_locations",
 13747	                       static_cast<size_t>( std::distance( stale, picked_up_stuff.end() ) ) );
 13748	        picked_up_stuff.erase( stale, picked_up_stuff.end() );
 13749	    }
 13750	    // Carrier removal can trigger restacking, invalidating later item_locations.
 13751	    // The get_item() check catches this - items are likely merged on the carrier
 13752	    // rather than lost, but are skipped by return logic.
 13753	    // Fixing this requires reworking item_location lifetime guarantees.
 13754	    int return_failures = 0;
 13755	    for( item_location &item : picked_up_stuff ) {
 13756	        if( item.get_item() ) {
 13757	            // Skip virtual pickup items still at source - they were never moved.
 13758	            if( const vehicle_cursor *vc = item.veh_cursor() ) {
 13759	                if( vc->veh.abs_part_pos( vc->part ) == get_map().get_abs( src_bub ) ) {
 13760	                    continue;
 13761	                }
 13762	            }
 13763	            // Place the item back at the source tile
 13764	            std::vector<item_location> returned = put_into_vehicle_or_drop_ret_locs(
 13765	                    you, item_drop_reason::deliberate, { *item }, src_bub );
 13766	            if( returned.empty() ) {
 13767	                // Placement failed - keep item on carrier rather than silently destroying it.
 13768	                add_msg_debug( debugmode::DF_ACTIVITY,
 13769	                               "zone_sort return_items: failed to place '%s' at source, keeping on carrier",
 13770	                               item->tname() );

 13388	void zone_activity_actor::do_turn( player_activity &act, Character &you )
 13389	{
 13390	    update_vehicle_zone_cache();
 13391	
 13392	    //Prepare activity stage
 13393	    if( stage == UNINIT ) {
 13394	        stage = INIT;
 13395	    }
 13396	    if( stage == INIT ) {
 13397	        stage_init( act, you );
 13398	    }
 13399	    if( stage == THINK ) {
 13400	        add_msg_debug( debugmode::DF_ACTIVITY,
 13401	                       "zone_sort do_turn: THINK pos=(%d,%d) has_dest=%d coords=%zu",
 13402	                       you.pos_bub().x(), you.pos_bub().y(),
 13403	                       you.has_destination() ? 1 : 0,
 13404	                       coord_set.size() );
 13405	        if( !stage_think( act, you ) ) {
 13406	            return;
 13407	        }
 13408	    }
 13409	    if( stage == DO ) {
 13410	        //to end this activity, THINK stage must resolve all zone tiles
 13411	        stage_do( act, you );
 13412	        return;
 13413	    }
 13414	    // If we got here without restarting the activity, it means we're done
 13415	    add_msg( m_info, _( "%s sorted out every item possible." ), you.disp_name( false, true ) );
 13416	    if( you.is_npc() ) {
 13417	        npc *guy = dynamic_cast<npc *>( &you );
 13418	        guy->revert_after_activity();
 13419	    }
 13420	    act.set_to_null();
 13421	}

 13425	    //unused, zone activities do not have precalculated moves
 13426	}

 13435	    }

    98	/*
    99	* General forwarding activity to handle single-tile activities performed in zones.
   100	* This forwarding works in two ways:
   101	* 1) when travelling to a destination zone tile, storing this multi-activity in Character::destination_activity,
   102	*    which is restarted when the player reaches the destination
   103	* 2) when doing the single-tile activity, backlogging a copy of the multi-activity actor in Character::backlog,
   104	*    which is then restarted from the single-tile activity with activity_handlers::resume_for_multi_activities()
   105	* An important point to note is that in no case does multi_zone_activity_actor store state.

2026-05-28 15:26:00,712 - INFO - swea-agent - 💭 THOUGHT
Now let me understand the flow better. The issue is:

1. Player presses 'O' to sort items.
2. The `stage_init` finds unsorted zones and sets up the viewport to center on them.
3. If no items can be sorted (because items aren't in the unsorted zone, or there's no destination zone for them), the activity should end and restore the viewport.
4. But the viewport doesn't get restored properly.

Let me look at the `do_turn` flow more carefully:

In `zone_activity_actor::do_turn` (lines 13388-13421):
- If stage == UNINIT → INIT
- If stage == INIT → stage_init (sets up coord_set, viewport)
- If stage == THINK → stage_think
- If stage == DO → stage_do

If we reach the end without restarting, it says "sorted out every item possible" and calls `act.set_to_null()`.

Now look at `zone_sort_activity_actor::do_turn` (lines 13455-13476):
```cpp
void zone_sort_activity_actor::do_turn(player_activity &act, Character &you) {
    update_other_activity_items();
    const bool had_viewport = viewport_was_active;
    const int saved_zoom = viewport_saved_zoom;
    zone_activity_actor::do_turn(act, you);
    
    // True completion: activity nulled AND no auto-move destination pending.
    if (act.is_null() && !you.has_destination() && had_viewport) {
        if (!test_mode) {
            g->set_zoom(saved_zoom);
            g->mark_main_ui_adaptor_resize();
        }
        if (you.is_avatar()) {
            you.as_avatar()->zone_sort_viewport = {};
        }
    }
}
```

So the viewport is restored when:
1. `act.is_null()` - activity is nulled
2. `!you.has_destination()` - no destination pending
3. `had_viewport` - viewport was active

Now look at `stage_think` (lines 13537-13734):
- It iterates through unsorted zone positions
- For each, checks if there are items to sort via `has_items_to_sort`
- If there are items to sort, it routes to the source and returns false (to stop processing for this turn)
- If there are no items to sort at a particular source, it erases it from coord_set and continues

The critical part: when `coord_set` is empty after removing all sources with no items, the loop ends. Then at line 13727-13732:
```cpp
if (stage != DO && !coord_set.empty()) {
    coord_set.clear();
}
return true;
```

If `stage != DO` (i.e., still THINK) and `coord_set` is empty, it returns true. Then back in `do_turn`:
- stage is still THINK (not DO)
- It falls through to "sorted out every item possible" and sets `act.set_to_null()`

But wait - if `coord_set` was empty from the beginning (no unsorted zones at all), stage_init would find nothing, coord_set is empty, and then in stage_think, the loop over src_sorted would be empty, stage would remain THINK, and it would return true. Then the base do_turn would say "sorted out every item possible" and null the activity.

But the viewport... In `stage_init` (line 13512):
```cpp
if (you.is_avatar() && !coord_set.empty()) {
    viewport_was_active = true;
    ...
}
```

So if `coord_set` is empty, `viewport_was_active` stays false, and the restore in do_turn won't fire. That seems fine for that case.

But what about the case where `coord_set` IS non-empty (there ARE unsorted zones), but no items can be sorted?

In that case:
1. `stage_init` sets up viewport (viewport_was_active = true)
2. `stage_think` iterates through sources, finds no items to sort, erases all from coord_set
3. Returns true (stage is still THINK)
4. Back in `do_turn`, falls through to "sorted out every item possible", sets `act.set_to_null()`
5. In `zone_sort_activity_actor::do_turn`, checks `act.is_null() && !you.has_destination() && had_viewport` → should restore viewport

This should work... unless there's a case where the activity gets nulled AND a destination is set. Let me look at another path.

Wait, let me check the `stage_do` function:

 13794	void zone_sort_activity_actor::stage_do( player_activity &act, Character &you )
 13795	{
 13796	    map &here = get_map();
 13797	    const zone_manager &mgr = zone_manager::get_manager();
 13798	
 13799	    const faction_id fac_id = you.get_faction_id();
 13800	    const bool skip_personal = !you.is_avatar() && mgr.has_personal_zones();
 13801	    const tripoint_abs_ms src( placement );
 13802	    const tripoint_bub_ms src_bub = here.get_bub( src );
 13803	    const tripoint_abs_ms abspos = you.pos_abs();
 13804	
 13805	    add_msg_debug( debugmode::DF_ACTIVITY,
 13806	                   "zone_sort DO: pos=(%d,%d) src=(%d,%d,%d) has_dest=%d picked=%zu dropoffs=%zu",
 13807	                   you.pos_bub().x(), you.pos_bub().y(),
 13808	                   src.x(), src.y(), src.z(),
 13809	                   you.has_destination() ? 1 : 0,
 13810	                   picked_up_stuff.size(), dropoff_coords.size() );
 13811	
 13812	    // Dropping off
 13813	    if( !dropoff_coords.empty() && !picked_up_stuff.empty() ) {
 13814	        auto dest_iter = dropoff_coords.begin();
 13815	        while( dest_iter != dropoff_coords.end() ) {
 13816	            const tripoint_abs_ms drop_dest = *dest_iter;
 13817	            // Sometimes we loop back to here while still picking stuff up (because we spent all our moves picking up)
 13818	            // Don't start trying to teleport-undrop at the destination until we're actually adjacent.
 13819	            const bool is_adjacent_or_closer_to_dest = square_dist( abspos, drop_dest ) <= 1;
 13820	
 13821	            if( is_adjacent_or_closer_to_dest ) {
 13822	                auto iter = picked_up_stuff.begin();
 13823	                // ensure validity of all item_locations before we start this - if any have been invalidated there's a bug somewhere earlier
 13824	                for( item_location itm_loc : picked_up_stuff ) {
 13825	                    if( !itm_loc.get_item() ) {
 13826	                        debugmsg( "Lost item_location during sorting" );
 13827	                        stage = LAST;
 13828	                        return;
 13829	                    }
 13830	                }
 13831	                while( iter != picked_up_stuff.end() ) {
 13832	                    if( you.get_moves() <= 0 ) { // Ran out of moves dropping stuff
 13833	                        return;
 13834	                    }
 13835	
 13836	                    // Place item at destination directly. Zone binding
 13837	                    // determines the fallback chain: vehicle-only zones skip
 13838	                    // ground, everything else tries cargo then ground.
 13839	                    const zone_type_id drop_zt = mgr.get_near_zone_type_for_item( **iter,
 13840	                                                 drop_dest, 0, fac_id );
 13841	                    const bool vehicle_only = drop_zt != zone_type_id::NULL_ID() &&
 13842	                                              mgr.has_vehicle( drop_zt, drop_dest, fac_id ) &&
 13843	                                              !mgr.has_terrain( drop_zt, drop_dest, fac_id );
 13844	                    bool placed = false;
 13845	                    const tripoint_bub_ms drop_bub = here.get_bub( drop_dest );
 13846	                    if( std::optional<vpart_reference> ovp = here.veh_at( drop_bub ).cargo() ) {
 13847	                        item copy( **iter );
 13848	                        if( ovp->vehicle().add_item( here, ovp->part(), copy ) ) {
 13849	                            placed = true;
 13850	                        }
 13851	                    }
 13852	                    if( !placed && !vehicle_only ) {
 13853	                        item copy( **iter );
 13854	                        item_location ground = here.add_item_or_charges_ret_loc(
 13855	                                                   drop_bub, copy, false );
 13856	                        placed = ground.get_item() != nullptr;
 13857	                    }
 13858	                    if( placed ) {
 13859	                        you.mod_moves( -you.item_handling_cost( **iter ) );
 13860	                        if( const vehicle_cursor *veh_curs = iter->veh_cursor() ) {
 13861	                            vehicle &cart_with_items = veh_curs->veh;
 13862	                            cart_with_items.remove_item( cart_with_items.part( veh_curs->part ), iter->get_item() );
 13863	                        } else if( iter->carrier() ) {
 13864	                            iter->carrier()->i_rem( iter->get_item() );
 13865	                        }
 13866	                        iter = picked_up_stuff.erase( iter );
 13867	                        // dumb. Go through and clear our any now-invalidated item_locations. Then reset iter to the start, to *make sure* we iterate everything.
 13868	                        // Definitely wasteful, but I am not paid enough to figure out a better way.
 13869	                        auto cleanup_iter = picked_up_stuff.begin();
 13870	                        while( cleanup_iter != picked_up_stuff.end() ) {
 13871	                            if( !cleanup_iter->get_item() ) {
 13872	                                cleanup_iter = picked_up_stuff.erase( cleanup_iter );
 13873	                            } else {
 13874	                                cleanup_iter++;
 13875	                            }
 13876	                        }
 13877	                        // Again: Always reset to the start if we dropped stuff.
 13878	                        iter = picked_up_stuff.begin();
 13879	                    } else {
 13880	                        iter++; // Failed to drop for some reason?!
 13881	                    }
 13882	                }
 13883	                dest_iter = dropoff_coords.erase( dest_iter ); // Done dropping stuff here.
 13884	            } else {
 13885	                dest_iter++; // Evaluate next dropoff
 13886	            }
 13887	        }
 13888	
 13889	        // Items remain but no adjacent destinations. Route to nearest reachable
 13890	        // dropoff, trying closest-first. Skip if still at source (keep picking up).
 13891	        if( !picked_up_stuff.empty() && !dropoff_coords.empty() &&
 13892	            !you.has_destination() && square_dist( you.pos_bub(), src_bub ) > 1 ) {
 13893	            std::sort( dropoff_coords.begin(), dropoff_coords.end(),
 13894	            [&abspos]( const tripoint_abs_ms & a, const tripoint_abs_ms & b ) {
 13895	                return square_dist( abspos, a ) < square_dist( abspos, b );
 13896	            } );
 13897	
 13898	            bool routed = false;
 13899	            auto dest_it = dropoff_coords.begin();
 13900	            while( dest_it != dropoff_coords.end() ) {
 13901	                if( zone_sorting::route_to_destination( you, act, here.get_bub( *dest_it ), stage ) ) {
 13902	                    routed = true;
 13903	                    break;
 13904	                }
 13905	                dest_it = dropoff_coords.erase( dest_it );
 13906	            }
 13907	
 13908	            if( !routed ) {
 13909	                // All dropoff destinations unreachable after player left source.
 13910	                // Defensive: route_length populated dropoff_coords earlier, but
 13911	                // route_to_destination failed from the player's current position.
 13912	                // Both use the same A* in a single turn, so this can't fire normally.
 13913	                return_items_to_source( you, src_bub );
 13914	                unreachable_sources.emplace( src );
 13915	                stage = THINK;
 13916	                you.mod_moves( -1 );
 13917	            }
 13918	            return;
 13919	        }
 13920	    }
 13921	
 13922	    // Reset iteration state when a batch has been fully delivered.
 13923	    // Must happen before items.begin() + num_processed arithmetic below.
 13924	    if( picked_up_stuff.empty() ) {
 13925	        virtual_pickup_active = false;
 13926	        num_processed = 0;
 13927	    }
 13928	
 13929	    bool is_adjacent_or_closer = square_dist( you.pos_bub(), src_bub ) <= 1;
 13930	    // before we move any item, check if player is at or

Now let me look at the full flow more carefully. Let me re-read the `do_turn` functions:

In `zone_activity_actor::do_turn` (lines 13388-13421):
1. If stage == UNINIT → INIT
2. If stage == INIT → stage_init (sets up coord_set, viewport)
3. If stage == THINK → stage_think
4. If stage == DO → stage_do
5. If we reach the end without restarting, print "sorted out every item possible" and null activity

Then in `zone_sort_activity_actor::do_turn` (lines 13455-13476):
- It saves `had_viewport` and `saved_zoom`
- Calls base `do_turn`
- If `act.is_null() && !you.has_destination() && had_viewport` → restores viewport

Now, the key issue: what happens when we get to the end of `do_turn` and the activity is nulled? The viewport should be restored. But let me check if there's a case where the activity gets interrupted/aborted without going through this path.

Actually, wait. Let me re-read the PR description more carefully:

> When trying to sort items, I had the items to be sorted on the wrong tile that is not within the Unsorted Zone. I pressed (O) to sort all items, and the view became stuck within the center where the Unsorted Zone was when I started the action.

So the issue is:
1. There IS an unsorted zone somewhere
2. Items are NOT in the unsorted zone
3. Player presses 'O' to sort
4. Camera centers on the unsorted zone (since `stage_init` sets up the viewport)
5. No items get sorted (because items aren't in the unsorted zone)
6. The activity ends (because `has_items_to_sort` returns false for all sources, and coord_set gets emptied)
7. BUT the viewport is NOT restored

Let me trace through what happens in this case:

1. `stage_init`: Finds unsorted zones, sets `viewport_was_active = true`, sets `coord_set` with those zones
2. `stage_think`: Iterates through sources. For each source, calls `has_items_to_sort`. If the source has no items (because items aren't on that tile), `has_items_to_sort` returns false (because `src_has_unsorted` is true but the loop would need to iterate items... wait, let me check this).

Actually, let me look at `has_items_to_sort` more carefully. At line 1087-1088:
```cpp
for (std::pair<item *, bool> it_pair : items) {
    if (!src_has_unsorted) {
        continue;
    }
```

If `src_has_unsorted` is true (there IS an unsorted zone at src), then it goes through each item, checks for destination zones. If there are no items at all, the loop finishes and returns false. If there ARE items (maybe from other sources like the player's inventory?), but no destination zones match, it also returns false.

Actually wait, the items come from `populate_items(src_bub)` which gets items at that tile only. If the unsorted zone tile has no items, `has_items_to_sort` returns false.

So if there's an unsorted zone but no items on that tile:
- `has_items_to_sort` returns false
- `coord_set.erase(src)` removes it
- Eventually all sources are removed
- Loop ends, `stage != DO && coord_set.empty()` → coord_set cleared, returns true
- Back in `do_turn`: falls through to "sorted out every item possible" message and `act.set_to_null()`
- Back in `zone_sort_activity_actor::do_turn`: checks `act.is_null() && !you.has_destination() && had_viewport` → should restore viewport

So it seems like it should work... unless there's another path I'm missing.

Let me look at `character.cpp` line 7195 more carefully. The function `abort_automove` is called when the player manually cancels auto-move. But what about when the player manually cancels the activity?

Let me also look at `canceled`:

```cpp
void zone_sort_activity_actor::canceled(player_activity &, Character &you) {
    restore_viewport(you);
}
```

And `restore_viewport`:
```cpp
void zone_sort_activity_actor::restore_viewport(Character &you) {
    if (!viewport_was_active || !you.is_avatar()) {
        return;
    }
    if (!test_mode) {
        g->set_zoom(viewport_saved_zoom);
        g->mark_main_ui_adaptor_resize();
    }
    you.as_avatar()->zone_sort_viewport = {};
    viewport_was_active = false;
}
```

This clears `zone_sort_viewport` which is the critical thing for the camera to follow the player again.

Now let me think about what scenario would cause the viewport to remain stuck. Let me check if there's a save/load issue. The PR description says the save is already prepared. Let me also check if there might be a case where the activity restarts and the viewport gets lost.

Actually, wait. Let me look at the `try_activate_zone_sort_viewport` function more carefully:

Now I see it! The `try_activate_zone_sort_viewport()` is called on every frame render. It reactivates the viewport based on the actor state. So even if the activity ends and clears `zone_sort_viewport` on the avatar, the next frame it would try to reactivate it.

But wait, `try_activate_zone_sort_viewport` checks if `u.zone_sort_viewport.active` is already true (line 6437) and returns early. So if it was cleared, it would check if there's still an active ACT_MOVE_LOOT activity. If the activity was nulled, it won't find an actor and will return without reactivating.

Let me trace the exact flow for the bug scenario again:

1. Player presses 'O' → `loot()` → `player_character.assign_activity(zone_sort_activity_actor())` 
2. Next turn: `zone_activity_actor::start` called (sets moves)
3. Then `zone_activity_actor::do_turn` called
4. Stage UNINIT → INIT
5. `stage_init` called:
   - Finds unsorted zones, populates `coord_set`
   - Sets `viewport_was_active = true`, saves zoom
   - Sets `zone_sort_viewport` on avatar
6. Stage THINK
7. `stage_think` called:
   - For each source in coord_set:
     - Gets items at source
     - Calls `has_items_to_sort` → returns false (no items at that tile)
     - Erases source from coord_set
   - After loop: `stage != DO && coord_set.empty()` → clears coord_set, returns true
8. Base `do_turn` continues: falls through to "sorted out every item possible", `act.set_to_null()`
9. `zone_sort_activity_actor::do_turn`: `act.is_null() && !you.has_destination() && had_viewport` → restores viewport

This should work! But maybe there's a case where the activity is NOT nulled? Let me check if there's another path...

Actually, let me look at what happens when `has_items_to_sort` returns true but then something goes wrong. Let me re-read `has_items_to_sort` more carefully.

Wait, I need to look at the condition at line 1087-1088 more carefully:

```cpp
for (std::pair<item *, bool> it_pair : items) {
    if (!src_has_unsorted) {
        continue;
    }
```

If `src_has_unsorted` is false (no unsorted zone at the source), it skips all items and returns false. But we know there IS an unsorted zone because `stage_init` found it. So `src_has_unsorted` should be true.

But wait: what if the unsorted zone exists, but the items that the player wants to sort are on a DIFFERENT tile? The player is standing on a tile with items, but that tile is NOT marked as unsorted zone. The unsorted zone is somewhere else.

In that case:
1. `stage_init` finds the unsorted zone tile(s)
2. `stage_think` iterates through those unsorted zone tiles
3. For each unsorted zone tile, it calls `has_items_to_sort` which checks items at THAT tile
4. If that tile has no items, returns false
5. Source erased from coord_set
6. Eventually coord_set empty

That seems fine and should restore the viewport. So what's going wrong?

Let me look more carefully at the condition where `has_items_to_sort` returns true. What if the unsorted zone tile HAS items but there's no destination zone for them?

At line 1096-1101:
```cpp
const zone_type_id dest_zone_type_id = mgr.get_near_zone_type_for_item(*it, abspos,
                                       MAX_VIEW_DISTANCE, fac_id);

if (dest_zone_type_id == zone_type_id::NULL_ID()) {
    continue;
}
```

If there's no destination zone, it continues to next item. If all items have no destination, the loop ends and returns false. Then `coord_set.erase(src)` removes it. Eventually coord_set empty, activity ends, viewport restored.

Now what if `has_items_to_sort` returns TRUE (there are items AND there's a destination), but then in `stage_do` something goes wrong?

Actually, let me look at the scenario from the PR description more carefully:

> After further testing, I found that this also happens when sorting items that are inside the Unsorted Zone but do not have any defined zones that fit them. For example, if there is only a Spare Parts zone but no Books or Default zone, it will sort planks correctly. However, if you try to sort a book without a specific Books or Default zone, the issue occurs even if the item is inside the Unsorted Zone.

So the scenario is:
1. There's an unsorted zone
2. There's a spare parts zone
3. There are planks (which go to spare parts) AND books (which have no destination)
4. Player presses 'O'

In this case:
- `stage_init`: finds unsorted zone, sets up coord_set with it, sets viewport
- `stage_think`: 
  - For the unsorted zone tile:
    - `has_items_to_sort` checks items:
      - Books: `dest_zone_type_id` = null (no matching zone) → continue
      - Planks: `dest_zone_type_id` = LOOT_WOOD (or similar) → has destination → returns true!
    - So `has_items_to_sort` returns true
    - Routes to source, stage = DO
- `stage_do`:
  - Processes items
  - For planks: finds destination, picks up, moves to destination, drops
  - For books: `zt_id` is NULL_ID, so at line 14008-14011:
    ```cpp
    if (zt_id == zone_type_id::NULL_ID()) {
        // After unloading, this item isn't going anywhere else.
        continue;
    }
    ```
  - So books are skipped

But wait, what if the book CAN be unloaded? Look at lines 1129-1155 in `has_items_to_sort`:
```cpp
if (zone_unload_options.unload_all || (zone_unload_options.unload_corpses && it->is_corpse())) {
    if (dest_set.empty() || zone_unload_options.unload_always) {
        if (you.rate_action_unload(*it) == hint_rating::good && !it->any_pockets_sealed()) {
            return true;
        }
        ...
    }
}
```

If the zone has unload options enabled, items that can be unloaded would cause `has_items_to_sort` to return true even without a destination. After unloading, if `zt_id` is NULL_ID, the item is skipped (not picked up).

Hmm, let me think about this differently. Let me look at the actual test file to understand the expected behavior:

  2538	    // Layout:
  2539	    //   source (UNSORTED) at (60,60)
  2540	    //   dest (PFOOD) at (60,55) -- 5 tiles north
  2541	    //   player starts at (60,61)
  2542	    const tripoint_bub_ms start_pos( 60, 61, 0 );
  2543	    dummy.setpos( here, start_pos );
  2544	    dummy.clear_destination();
  2545	    dummy.zone_sort_viewport = avatar::zone_sort_viewport_t{};
  2546	
  2547	    const tripoint_bub_ms src_pos( 60, 60, 0 );
  2548	    here.ter_set( src_pos, ter_t_floor );
  2549	    create_tile_zone( "Unsorted", zone_type_LOOT_UNSORTED, here.get_abs( src_pos ) );
  2550	
  2551	    const tripoint_bub_ms dest_pos( 60, 55, 0 );
  2552	    here.ter_set( dest_pos, ter_t_floor );
  2553	    create_tile_zone( "PFood", zone_type_LOOT_PFOOD, here.get_abs( dest_pos ) );
  2554	
  2555	    here.add_item_or_charges( src_pos, item( itype_test_apple ) );
  2556	
  2557	    here.invalidate_map_cache( 0 );
  2558	    here.build_map_cache( 0, true );
  2559	
  2560	    SECTION( "activates on sort start and deactivates on completion" ) {
  2561	        CHECK_FALSE( dummy.zone_sort_viewport.active );
  2562	
  2563	        dummy.assign_activity( zone_sort_activity_actor() );
  2564	
  2565	        // Run one turn to trigger stage_init
  2566	        dummy.mod_moves( dummy.get_speed() );
  2567	        while( dummy.get_moves() > 0 && dummy.activity ) {
  2568	            dummy.activity.do_turn( dummy );
  2569	        }
  2570	
  2571	        // Viewport should be active after init
  2572	        CHECK( dummy.zone_sort_viewport.active );
  2573	
  2574	        // Run to completion using teleport pattern
  2575	        int teleports = 0;
  2576	        const int max_teleports = 30;
  2577	        do {
  2578	            int turns = 0;
  2579	            while( dummy.activity && turns < 200 ) {
  2580	                dummy.mod_moves( dummy.get_speed() );
  2581	                while( dummy.get_moves() > 0 && dummy.activity ) {
  2582	                    dummy.activity.do_turn( dummy );
  2583	                }
  2584	                turns++;
  2585	            }
  2586	            if( dummy.destination_point ) {
  2587	                dummy.setpos( here, here.get_bub( *dummy.destination_point ) );
  2588	                if( dummy.has_destination_activity() ) {
  2589	                    dummy.start_destination_activity();
  2590	                } else {
  2591	                    dummy.clear_destination();
  2592	                }
  2593	                teleports++;
  2594	            }
  2595	        } while( ( dummy.activity || dummy.destination_point ) && teleports < max_teleports );
  2596	
  2597	        REQUIRE( teleports < max_teleports );
  2598	
  2599	        // Viewport should be deactivated after completion
  2600	        CHECK_FALSE( dummy.zone_sort_viewport.active );
  2601	    }
  2602	
  2603	    SECTION( "stays active during auto-move leg" ) {
  2604	        dummy.assign_activity( zone_sort_activity_actor() );
  2605	
  2606	        // Run until activity goes null (route_to_destination triggers auto-move)
  2607	        int turns = 0;
  2608	        while( dummy.activity && turns < 200 ) {
  2609	            dummy.mod_moves( dummy.get_speed() );
  2610	            while( dummy.get_moves() > 0 && dummy.activity ) {
  2611	                dummy.activity.do_turn( dummy );
  2612	            }
  2613	            turns++;
  2614	        }
  2615	
  2616	        // If we have a destination, we're in an auto-move leg
  2617	        if( dummy.destination_point ) {
  2618	            // Viewport should still be active during the leg
  2619	            CHECK( dummy.zone_sort_viewport.active );
  2620	        }
  2621	    }
  2622	
  2623	    SECTION( "abort_automove restores viewport" ) {
  2624	        dummy.assign_activity( zone_sort_activity_actor() );
  2625	
  2626	        // Run until auto-move starts
  2627	        int turns = 0;
  2628	        while( dummy.activity && turns < 200 ) {
  2629	            dummy.mod_moves( dummy.get_speed() );
  2630	            while( dummy.get_moves() > 0 && dummy.activity ) {
  2631	                dummy.activity.do_turn( dummy );
  2632	            }
  2633	            turns++;
  2634	        }
  2635	
  2636	        if( dummy.destination_point ) {
  2637	            CHECK( dummy.zone_sort_viewport.active );
  2638	            dummy.abort_automove();
  2639	            CHECK_FALSE( dummy.zone_sort_viewport.active );
  2640	        }
  2641	    }
  2642	
  2643	    SECTION( "viewport_saved_zoom survives serialize roundtrip" ) {
  2644	        dummy.assign_activity( zone_sort_activity_actor() );
  2645	
  2646	        // Run one turn to trigger stage_init
  2647	        dummy.mod_moves( dummy.get_speed() );
  2648	        while( dummy.get_moves() > 0 && dummy.activity ) {
  2649	            dummy.activity.do_turn( dummy );
  2650	        }
  2651	
  2652	        REQUIRE( dummy.zone_sort_viewport.active );
  2653	
  2654	        // Serialize and deserialize the activity
  2655	        const zone_sort_activity_actor *actor =
  2656	            dynamic_cast<const zone_sort_activity_actor *>( dummy.activity.actor.get() );
  2657	        if( actor ) {
  2658	            CHECK( actor->viewport_was_active );
  2659	            CHECK( actor->viewport_saved_zoom == 16 );  // DEFAULT_TILESET_ZOOM
  2660	        }

Lines 13727-13732:
```cpp
if (stage != DO && !coord_set.empty()) {
    // Remaining entries are filtered by unreachable_sources, have
    // full destinations, or were past the route probe cutoff.
    // Clear so the activity ends cleanly instead of re-scanning.
    coord_set.clear();
}
return true;
```

Wait, this says: if stage != DO AND coord_set is NOT empty, clear it. So if coord_set IS empty (because all sources were erased), it just returns true without clearing (it's already empty). The activity then ends.

But what if `stage == DO`? Then it doesn't clear coord_set and returns true. Let me check what happens when stage == DO at this point...

If stage == DO, that means the previous iteration set stage = DO. In that case, `stage_think` returns true, then `do_turn` calls `stage_do`.

Now I see: `set_destination` stores the activity in `destination_activity`. Then when the player reaches the destination, `start_destination_activity()` calls `assign_activity(get_destination_activity())` which creates a COPY of the activity. The original actor's state (viewport_was_active, etc.) is preserved because it's serialized/copied.

Now let me look at `route_to_destination` (line 851-904):

```cpp
bool route_to_destination(Character &you, player_activity &act,
                          const tripoint_bub_ms &dest, zone_activity_stage &stage) {
    ...
    if (route.empty()) {
        add_msg(m_info, _("%s can't reach the source tile. ..."));
        return false;
    }
    stage = DO;
    you.set_destination(route, act);
    you.activity.set_to_null();
    return true;
}
```

So `route_to_destination` sets `stage = DO` (by reference), sets the destination, and nulls the current activity. The destination activity is the original activity (with the actor). Later, when the player arrives, `start_destination_activity` restores it.

But wait: the stage parameter is passed by reference and modified to DO. But then `act.set_to_null()` nulls the activity. The caller (`stage_think`) then returns false. The base `do_turn` sees stage == DO? No, wait...

```cpp

        ...

}
```

`route_to_destination` sets `stage = DO` and nulls the activity. Then `stage_think` returns false. Back in `do_turn`:
```cpp
if (stage == THINK) {
    ...
    if (!stage_think(act, you)) {
        return;
    }
}
if (stage == DO) {
    stage_do(act, you);
    return;
}
```

Wait! `route_to_destination` sets `stage = DO` as an output parameter. But this `stage` is the member variable `this->stage`. So yes, after `route_to_destination`, `stage` is DO. But the activity is nulled. Then `do_turn` checks `stage == DO` and calls `stage_do` on a nulled activity? That seems wrong.

Actually wait, `stage` is a reference parameter in `route_to_destination`, but it's `zone_activity_stage &stage`. In `stage_think`, it's passed to `route_to_destination` as `stage` which is `this->stage`. So yes, `this->stage` gets set to DO.

Then `stage_think` returns false. Then `do_turn` returns (line 13406). The activity is null, but stage is DO.

Next time `do_turn` is called... the activity is null, so `act.is_null()` is true. The while loop in the game probably stops calling do_turn when activity is null.

Actually wait, let me re-check. When `route_to_destination` is called:
1. It sets `you.set_destination(route, act)` - this saves the current activity (with the zone_sort_activity_actor) as the destination activity
2. It sets `you.activity.set_to_null()` - this clears the current activity

Now the game loop will see no activity and process movement. When the player reaches the destination, `start_destination_activity()` is called which calls `assign_activity(get_destination_activity())`. The destination activity is the saved copy of the zone_sort_activity_actor with `stage = DO` (since it was set before saving).

Wait, actually `set_destination` copies the activity BEFORE `stage` was modified to DO? No, `route_to_destination` sets `stage = DO` BEFORE calling `set_destination`. Let me re-read:

```cpp
stage = DO;
you.set_destination(route, act);
you.activity.set_to_null();
```

So `act` (the player's current activity) which has `stage` modified to DO is saved as the destination activity. Then the current activity is nulled.

When the player arrives, `start_destination_activity` calls `assign_activity(get_destination_activity())` which creates a new activity from the saved one. The actor is copied, and the copy has `stage = DO`.

Then `do_turn` is called on the new activity. `stage == DO`, so `stage_do` is called directly, bypassing `stage_init`. This is correct because the viewport was already set up before.

But wait: what if the destination activity's actor doesn't have `viewport_was_active` set? Let me check: `route_to_destination` modifies `stage` but doesn't touch `viewport_was_active`. The stage was set to DO, but `viewport_was_active` should still be true from the original `stage_init` call. Since the actor is copied, it should retain `viewport_was_active = true`.

So the flow seems correct for the normal case. But the bug report says the view gets stuck. Let me think about what could go wrong...
